description: You are a clinical pharmacist preparing a drug interaction safety briefing for an oncology multidisciplinary team (MDT) meeting. Three targeted cancer therapies are being considered for patients on the haematology-oncology ward: Ibrutinib, Venetoclax, and Crizotinib. All three patients take the same co-medications for common comorbidities: Ketoconazole (antifungal prophylaxis), Verapamil (atrial fibrillation rate control), and Warfarin (venous thromboembolism prophylaxis). Using the Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive app, check all nine cancer drug-co-medication interaction pairs and review the Interaction Details for any rated Red or Orange/Amber. Determine which cancer drug has the fewest Red interactions (alphabetical tiebreak if equal). For that drug, check whether substituting its Red-flagged co-medications with safer alternatives resolves the contraindications: Ketoconazole to Fluconazole, Verapamil to Bisoprolol, Warfarin to Apixaban. Remain on the Interaction Details page for the selected cancer drug paired with whichever substitute co-medication shows the most severe remaining interaction (severity order: Red > Orange > Yellow > Green > Grey). If no Red interactions existed for the selected drug, remain on its most severe original co-medication's Interaction Details page instead.

metadata.clinical_context: Pre-MDT polypharmacy drug interaction safety review across three cancer therapies
